# Casual Editor — Word-flavored web document editor

> Open-source, real-time collaborative web `.docx` editor. ProseMirror
> with an OOXML-preserving model (forked from `eigenpal/docx-editor`,
> MIT); React + Bun frontend; Go for the stateless WebSocket gateway
> implementing the `y-websocket` protocol. Document persistence is
> delegated to a pluggable host integration (inline for v0; WOPI /
> JWT-API later).

## Quick facts

- **License:** Apache-2.0 (gateway) + MIT (editor fork)
- **Repo:** https://github.com/CasualOffice/docs
- **Live demo:** https://docs.casualoffice.org/
- **Docker:** https://hub.docker.com/r/casualoffice/docs
- **Marketing site:** https://casualoffice.org/
- **Author:** Sachin Sarwa — https://casualoffice.org/
- **Status:** Public preview. 26 of 39 .docx fixtures round-trip
  pristine in the per-tag audit; working toward ≥ 90% before the
  desktop binary ships.

## What it does

- Opens and saves native `.docx` files in the browser with WYSIWYG
  fidelity. ProseMirror schema preserves OOXML structure; the layout
  painter ports Word's box model.
- Real-time multi-user co-editing via Yjs + y-prosemirror over a
  custom Go y-websocket implementation (~120 LOC). The server is
  STATELESS — no DB, no on-disk update log. State lives only in
  memory while a session is active; gone when the last client
  disconnects.
- Document persistence is owned by the host. Concrete inline impl
  ships for v0 (share-link self-contained model). WOPI / JWT-API
  integrations slot in behind one Go interface for v1+.
- 14-template home gallery with real first-page PNG previews
  rendered via LibreOffice. Four categories: Personal, Work,
  Education, Career.
- Word-compat heuristics behind an opt-in `wordCompat` flag for
  non-spec behaviour (e.g. last-row closing border, issue #395).
- Three-way fidelity harness compares our output vs LibreOffice and
  OnlyOffice DocumentBuilder; per-tag round-trip audit gates CI.

## Architecture

- **Frontend (`docx-editor/`):** React + TypeScript + Bun + Vite.
  ProseMirror schema + layout painter from the `eigenpal/docx-editor`
  fork. MIT-only — the upstream AGPL `agent-use` package is purged.
- **Realtime (`backend/`):** Go 1.24. Implements the y-websocket
  protocol in ~120 LOC. Room manager keeps one in-memory `Y.Doc`
  per live session, dropped when the last client disconnects.
- **Host abstraction (`backend/internal/host/`):** `Integration`
  interface with an inline impl for the v0 share-link flow.
  Pluggable for WOPI / JWT-secured REST API in v1+.
- **Snapshot worker (planned M2):** Bun worker pool that turns the
  live CRDT state into a fresh `.docx` on room drain. Currently the
  gateway re-serves the original upload.

## Sibling projects

Part of a three-project suite all by Sachin Sarwa:

- **Casual Editor** — this project — https://docs.casualoffice.org/
- **Casual Sheets** — Excel-flavored web spreadsheet —
  https://sheets.casualoffice.org/ — https://github.com/CasualOffice/sheets
- **Casual Desktop** — Tauri binaries reusing both web cores —
  in progress.

## Tech stack

- **Frontend:** TypeScript · React · ProseMirror · Bun · Vite · Yjs · y-prosemirror
- **Realtime + backend:** Go 1.24 · WebSocket · y-websocket protocol
- **Quality:** Playwright · GitHub Actions · LibreOffice fidelity harness · ESLint + Prettier
- **Ops:** Docker · GitHub Actions · GitHub Pages (single-user demo) · Docker Hub
